description: UI polish, export dialog fixes, infinite scroll, OG tags, resizable sidebar

userStories.title: Rename 'Name' to 'Flow Name' in export dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want the name field in the export dialog to be labelled 'Flow Name' so the label is more descriptive.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/web/src/components/export-dialog.tsx, the label for the name input reads 'Flow Name' (not 'Name')
The data-testid 'export-name-input' attribute is unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix Copy button in export dialog not copying the share link
userStories.description: As a user, after a successful export I want the Copy button to copy the share URL to my clipboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the Copy button (data-testid='export-copy') in the done state copies state.shareUrl to the clipboard
After clicking, the button briefly shows a checkmark (already in place) and reverts after 2s
The copy works without any browser permission errors (use navigator.clipboard.writeText with proper error handling)
If clipboard write fails, the button does not break silently — catch the error and log it
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Home page 'Discover recent flows' — white cards, shadow, Skeleton component
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the Discover recent flows section on the home page to show white cards with a subtle shadow and proper animated skeletons while loading.
acceptanceCriteria: Each FlowCard in the DiscoverSection on the home page has a white (#fff) background and a subtle box-shadow (e.g. '0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)')
The flow name text and date text are dark (readable on white background)
The MiniCanvas thumbnail area has a light background (#f8fafc or similar) instead of dark (#09090b)

Loading skeleton cards in DiscoverSection use the Skeleton component from apps/viewer's UI lib if one exists (check src/components/ui/skeleton.tsx or similar); if not, keep custom skeleton but animate it with a pulse/shimmer
The section heading 'Discover recent flows' and 'View all →' link remain unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: /flows page — max-width, responsive grid, 600px card cap, infinite scroll
userStories.description: As a user, I want the /flows discovery page to have a capped max-width, show an appropriate number of cards per screen size, cap each card at 600px, and load more flows automatically as I scroll.
acceptanceCriteria: The page content has a max-width of 1400px centered horizontally
Grid columns: 1 on mobile (<768px), 2 on md (768px–1199px), 3 on lg (≥1200px) — each column capped at 600px max-width
Initial fetch uses a limit of 20 flows (replace the const LIMIT = 12 with 20)
Pagination controls (Previous / Next buttons) are removed
Infinite scroll: when the user scrolls near the bottom (within ~300px), the next page is fetched and appended to the existing list
An IntersectionObserver sentinel div at the bottom of the list triggers the next-page fetch
A 'Loading more…' indicator appears below the grid while fetching the next page
When all pages are loaded, the sentinel shows 'All flows loaded' or is hidden
Error during load-more shows an inline 'Failed to load more' message with a retry option
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Ensure FlowCard MiniCanvas thumbnail renders correctly (not blank)
userStories.description: As a user, I want each flow card to show a live canvas preview of the flow diagram, not a blank or black box.
acceptanceCriteria: The MiniCanvas container inside FlowCard has an explicit height so ReactFlow receives a non-zero computed height (e.g. set height: '100%' on the parent and ensure the aspectRatio container propagates height correctly, or switch to a fixed pixel height derived from the card width)
MiniCanvas renders the flow nodes visually — not a blank/black box — for typical flows with at least one node
The MiniCanvas background uses a light color (#f8fafc) consistent with US-003 white card theme
No static image ( or background-image) is used for flow thumbnails; the live ReactFlow canvas is the only thumbnail
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Make viewer home page header logo a proper clickable link
userStories.description: As a user, I want the SeeFlow logo in the home page header to behave as a proper link so it is keyboard-accessible and right-clickable.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/viewer/src/pages/home.tsx, the logo '<div>' in the header is replaced with a '<Link to="/">' (from react-router-dom) or an '<a href="/">'
The logo has 'cursor: pointer' and a hover state (e.g. slight opacity change) to indicate it is interactive
The ViewerHeader logo (on /flows and /flow/:uuid routes) is already a Link — no change needed there
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Open Graph meta tags to viewer flow pages for social sharing
userStories.description: As a user sharing a flow link, I want chat apps and search engines to display a rich preview with the flow name and description.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/viewer/index.html has static baseline OG tags: og:site_name='SeeFlow', og:type='website', og:description, og:url, twitter:card='summary_large_image'
Install react-helmet-async (or use the existing package if already installed) in apps/viewer
Wrap the app in HelmetProvider in apps/viewer/src/main.tsx

apps/viewer/src/pages/flow-view.tsx uses Helmet to set: <title>{flowName} | SeeFlow</title>, <meta property='og:title'>, <meta property='og:description'> (e.g. 'Interactive architecture diagram on SeeFlow'), <meta property='og:url'> (current URL)
apps/viewer/src/pages/flows.tsx uses Helmet to set title 'Flows | SeeFlow'
apps/viewer/src/pages/home.tsx uses Helmet to set the default title 'SeeFlow | Architecture diagrams that actually run'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Make viewer detail panel (view-detail-panel) resizable by dragging
userStories.description: As a user viewing a flow, I want to drag the left edge of the detail panel to resize it, and have my preference remembered across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/viewer/src/lib/view-detail-panel-width.ts with constants VIEWER_DETAIL_PANEL_WIDTH_KEY='seeflow:viewer-detail-panel-width', DEFAULT=360, MIN=280, MAX=700 and helpers clamp, getStored, setStored, startResizeGesture — mirroring the pattern in apps/web/src/lib/detail-panel-width.ts
apps/viewer/src/components/view-detail-panel.tsx reads the stored width on mount via getStored and uses it as the panel width (replacing the hardcoded 360)
A 6px-wide drag handle div is rendered on the left edge of the panel (position: absolute, left: 0, top: 0, height: '100%', cursor: 'col-resize', zIndex: 11)
onPointerDown on the handle calls startResizeGesture; onWidth updates the panel width live; onCommit calls setStored
Width is clamped between MIN and MAX at all times
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Rename skill 'create-seeflow' to 'seeflow' and update all references
userStories.description: As a developer and user, I want the SeeFlow skill to be invoked as /seeflow (not /create-seeflow) and the skill directory renamed accordingly, with all references updated throughout the codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: Rename directory skills/create-seeflow/ to skills/seeflow/ (git mv)

skills/seeflow/SKILL.md: frontmatter name changed from 'create-seeflow' to 'seeflow'; all internal path references updated from 'skills/create-seeflow/' to 'skills/seeflow/'; trigger examples changed from '/create-seeflow' to '/seeflow'
.claude-plugin/plugin.json: 'name' field changed from 'create-seeflow' to 'seeflow'; 'agents' path changed from './skills/create-seeflow/agents/' to './skills/seeflow/agents/'
.cursor-plugin/plugin.json: same changes as .claude-plugin/plugin.json
README.md: '/plugin install create-seeflow@seeflow' → '/plugin install seeflow@seeflow'; '/create-seeflow show...' → '/seeflow show...'
apps/studio/README.md: same README.md changes
apps/viewer/src/pages/home.tsx: terminal mockup line changed from '/create-seeflow show me the shopping cart feature' to '/seeflow show me the shopping cart feature'
All agent .md files in skills/seeflow/agents/ updated to replace any 'create-seeflow' path references with 'seeflow' (e.g. 'skills/create-seeflow/...' → 'skills/seeflow/...')
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Redesign viewer homepage features section as alternating-row layout with mockups
userStories.description: As a visitor to the SeeFlow homepage, I want a clear, visually compelling features section where each feature gets its own full-width row with an animated HTML/CSS mockup and description, alternating left/right, replacing the existing bento grid.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Features Bento' section (id='features', currently a 3-column grid with 2 cards) in apps/viewer/src/pages/home.tsx is replaced with a 7-row alternating layout

A reusable FeatureRow component accepts: reverse?: boolean, label: string, heading: string, body: string, bullets?: string[], mockup: React.ReactNode — renders 2-col grid on lg+ (flex-row-reverse when reverse=true), stacks to single column on mobile
Row 1 (mockup LEFT): Interactive Canvas — animated node-firing-request mockup with Gateway and Payment nodes; heading 'Click a node. Fire a real request.'
Row 2 (mockup RIGHT, reverse): Live Status — streaming metric tickers mockup with 4 rows each showing label, value, and progress bar; heading 'Metrics streaming directly from your app.'

Row 3 (mockup LEFT): AI Agent — terminal mockup showing '/seeflow show me the checkout feature' with scanning output and 4 editor badges (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Codex); heading 'Zero to running demo in one prompt.'

Row 4 (mockup RIGHT, reverse): MCP — .mcp.json config snippet + 5 available tool names list (list_demos, get_demo, add_node, patch_connector, register_demo); heading 'Your flow becomes the architecture source.'
Row 5 (mockup LEFT): Share — export dialog mockup with name input, Public/Link-only toggle buttons, Publish button, and share URL row; heading 'Publish a live link in one click.'

Row 6 (mockup RIGHT, reverse): Git — terminal header showing 'git diff seeflow.json' with red/green diff lines and a footer note 'Committed, reviewed, reverted — just like code.'; heading 'Infrastructure that's committed and trackable.'

Row 7 (mockup LEFT): Open Source — LICENSE file mockup with repo/license info and 2x2 grid of facts (MIT, Free forever, Self-hosted, No account) plus a git clone command; heading '100% free. MIT licensed. No accounts.'
All mockup cards share dark rounded card style: border border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-950 rounded-xl shadow-xl overflow-hidden with a window chrome header
Rows are wrapped in a div with divide-y divide-zinc-800/50; each FeatureRow has py-12 md:py-20 vertical padding
The section heading 'Everything you need to document reality.' is preserved
The old bento grid (grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-4 with Interactive Canvas and Live Status cards) is removed entirely
No unused Lucide imports remain after the edit — remove MousePointer2 and Activity if they are no longer referenced elsewhere in the file
Typecheck passes
bun run format && bun run lint pass with no errors
Verify in browser: scroll to features section, confirm 7 rows render, alternating mockup sides visible on desktop, rows stack on mobile
userStories.notes: Full implementation plan at docs/plans/2026-05-17-homepage-features-section.md. Row 3 uses '/seeflow' (the renamed skill from US-009) — use '/seeflow' regardless of whether US-009 has already run.
